title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR pure-R parser binary .Q-View project file format used chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA plate imaging quantification. single .Q-View file bundles: plain-text manifest header, embedded H2 SQL database (project metadata, analyte panel, plate geometry, sample--well assignments, replicate pixel intensities, – user generated report inside producing application – fully rendered CSV report stored CLOB), one binary LOB segments holding raw chemiluminescent plate images. read_qview() extracts everything except raw images returns list tidy tibbles, Java runtime, H2 database driver, compiled code anywhere package.

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR requires R >= 4.1.0 plus small set tidyverse-aligned dependencies (cli, dplyr, lifecycle, openxlsx2, readr, rlang, tibble, tidyr). Plotting requires ggplot2 (Suggested); Shiny front-end additionally requires shiny, bslib, DT.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("CTTIR/qviewparsR")

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: end--end workflow short: read_qview() always returns list class qview eleven slots described ?read_qview. Empty slots zero-row tibbles rather NULL, downstream code can rely shape stability.
code: library(qviewparsR)  qv <- read_qview("path/to/plate.Q-View") qv                          # one-screen summary  qv$analytes                 # spot_number, analyte, unit, lod, lloq, uloq qv$well_groups              # one row per sample/calibrator/control qv$pixel_intensities        # long-format replicate readings qv$summary_statistics       # per-group mean / std-dev / CV rows qv$plate_layout             # one row per plate well  summary(qv)                 # mean / SD / CV per well type x analyte

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: producing software rewrites identifiers original well-assignment template CSV stores . mapping systematic reversible: strip_qview_prefix() reverses rewrite. Pass strip_prefix = TRUE read_qview() apply across every sample-id column : vectorised helper also useful :
code: qv <- read_qview("path/to/plate.Q-View", strip_prefix = TRUE) unique(qv$well_groups$sample_id) strip_qview_prefix(c("ICal 1", "GLow", "HHigh", "NFD24277364")) #> [1] "Cal 1"      "Low"        "High"       "FD24277364"

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: as_tibble() returns long-format pixel_intensities table – primary tabular payload – parsed object can drop straight dplyr / ggplot2 pipeline: is_qview() predicate lets package-aware functions guard inputs:
code: library(dplyr) library(tibble)  qv |>   as_tibble() |>   filter(replicate == 1L) |>   group_by(analyte, unit) |>   summarise(median_pi = median(pixel_intensity, na.rm = TRUE),             .groups = "drop") is_qview(qv)      # TRUE is_qview(list())  # FALSE

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Three quick-look plot types built (ggplot2 required): call returns ggplot object, themes, scales, labels can added top:
code: plot(qv, type = "plate_map")          # 96-well plate, fill = well type plot(qv, type = "intensity_heatmap")  # facet per analyte, fill = PI plot(qv, type = "replicate_scatter")  # rep 1 vs rep 2 per analyte library(ggplot2) plot(qv, type = "plate_map") +   theme_bw(base_size = 12) +   labs(title = NULL, subtitle = "QC overview")

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Three writers cover common destinations. return parsed object invisibly, compose |>: Add overwrite = TRUE write_qview_xlsx() / write_qview_rds() replace existing destinations. legacy aliases qview_to_xlsx() qview_to_csv_dir() still work flagged lifecycle::deprecate_warn() removed future release.
code: qv |>   write_qview_xlsx("plate.xlsx") |>      # one sheet per parsed table   write_qview_csv ("plate_csv/") |>      # one CSV file per parsed table   write_qview_rds ("plate.rds")          # full lossless R round-trip

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Every well assignment already embedded Q-View file. also original well-assignment template CSV producing application imported, read_qview_template() parses tibble aligns qv$plate_layout cross-validation: rows surviving filter expose template-vs-Q-View mismatches.
code: tmpl <- read_qview_template("path/to/template.csv")  qv$plate_layout |>   dplyr::left_join(tmpl, by = "well", suffix = c("_qview", "_template")) |>   dplyr::filter(sample_id_qview != sample_id_template)

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: non-coding collaborators, qview_app() launches small Shiny application workflow exposed visually: app accepts .Q-View upload (optionally template CSV), shows every parsed table three plots tabs, offers one-click downloads xlsx, rds, zipped CSV directory.
code: qview_app()

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Every exported function validates inputs early raises structured cli::cli_abort() error points user’s call, internal helpers. Typical shapes may see: messages carry bullet whenever actionable hint.
code: Error in `read_qview()`: ! `path` must be an existing file. x "missing.Q-View" does not exist. Error in `read_qview()`: ! `path` is not a valid `.Q-View` project file. x "junk.bin" is missing the expected container header. i Expected a numeric container version followed by "Q-View Project".

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: ?read_qview – exhaustive description every output slot. ?write_qview – three exporters share one help page. ?summary.qview – detail per-well-type aggregation. ?qview_app – launching interactive app. inst/extdata/ directory ship Q-View fixture binary format large; instead, test suite skips cleanly fixture present (tests/testthat/test-read_qview.R documents lookup paths).

title: qviewparsR
code: library(qviewparsR)  qv <- read_qview("path/to/project.Q-View") qv                                # compact summary qv$analytes                       # spot_number, analyte, unit, lod, lloq, ... qv$pixel_intensities              # long-format replicate readings qv$plate_layout                   # one row per well  plot(qv, type = "plate_map")      # plate visualisation (needs ggplot2) plot(qv, type = "intensity_heatmap") plot(qv, type = "replicate_scatter")  # Export (pipe-friendly: each writer returns qv invisibly) qv |>   write_qview_xlsx("out.xlsx") |>   write_qview_csv("out_csv/")  |>   write_qview_rds("out.rds")  # Per-analyte mean / SD / CV per well-type group summary(qv)  # Interactive front-end (upload, visualise, download) qview_app()

title: qviewparsR
text: .Q-View file single-file container bundles embedded H2 SQL database (Java, version 0.5/B) binary LOB files holding chemiluminescent plate images. file ZIP archive, XML, CSV — proprietary binary container plain-text manifest header followed concatenated H2 segments.

title: qviewparsR
text: H2 segment starts -- H2 0.5/B -- triplet marker. database uses 2048-byte pages.
code: +-----------------------------------------+ | Bytes 0 - ~290:  text manifest header   | |   - container version                   | |   - declared file entries (size + name) | +-----------------------------------------+ | Segment 1: main H2 SQL database         | |   - 36 tables (see schema below)        | |   - the rendered CSV report (CLOB)      | +-----------------------------------------+ | Segment 2: LOB file 1 (image data)      | +-----------------------------------------+ | Segment 3: LOB file 2 (more LOB data)   | +-----------------------------------------+

title: qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR recovers data embedded CSV report; table diagram documents underlying schema reference.

text: Q-View imports well-assignment template CSV prefixes identifiers internally: strip_qview_prefix() reverses transformation, read_qview(path, strip_prefix = TRUE) applies across whole returned object.

title: Plot a Q-View object — plot.qview

"intensity_heatmap" – per-analyte facet, fill replicate-1 pixel intensity per well. "replicate_scatter" – replicate 1 vs replicate 2 pixel intensity per analyte. Requires ggplot2 package (Suggested).

what: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
title: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
text: Pure-R parser .Q-View binary project files used chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA plate imaging quantification. Extracts pixel intensities, analyte mappings, sample assignments, plate layout, optional curve-fit parameters, embedded CSV report H2 database container format. Returns tidy tibbles ready downstream statistical analysis.

title: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
text: Reader: read_qview() – parse .Q-View file. Helpers: strip_qview_prefix() reverse Q-View internal naming convention; well_label() map (row, column) plate notation. Optional: read_qview_template() parse Q-View well-assignment template CSV. Methods: is_qview(), print.qview(), plot.qview(), as_tibble.qview(). Shiny: qview_app() interactive upload / preview / download.

title: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
text: Maintainer: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Marco Mannes

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
code: read_qview(   path,   strip_prefix = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
text: path Character. Path .Q-View file. strip_prefix Logical. TRUE, reverse Q-View's internal naming convention via strip_qview_prefix() identifiers match original well-assignment template. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Print short summary parsing. Default TRUE. call execution environment calling function. Used error reporting; experts .

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
text: list class "qview" containing: metadata Named list: project, plate, image, imager, product, user, report_created, qview_version, template, container_version, file_path, parsed_at. manifest Tibble one row per declared file entry (name, size_bytes, parent). segments Tibble H2 segment byte ranges (segment, start, end, size). analytes Tibble: spot_number, analyte, unit, plus lod, lloq, uloq, assay_control_low, assay_control_high reported. well_groups Tibble: well_group, sample_id, is_standard, is_negative, is_sample, is_control, well_type. pixel_intensities Long-format tibble replicate readings: well_group, sample_id, well, replicate, analyte, unit, pixel_intensity, dilution. summary_statistics Long-format tibble per-group averages, std-dev, CV statistics: well_group, sample_id, statistic, analyte, value, unit. concentrations Long-format concentration tibble NULL regression model "Qualitative". curve_fit Tibble analyte, regression_model, NULL reported. report_csv Character vector raw CSV report lines, NULL report generated. plate_layout Tibble one row per well: well, plate_row, plate_col, well_group, sample_id, well_type, dilution.

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
text: Parses .Q-View binary container (chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA project file holding embedded H2 database plus binary LOB segments) extracts assay data: project metadata, analyte panel units, well-group sample assignments, per-well replicate pixel intensities, summary statistics, (present) embedded CSV report. file format reverse-engineered public binary inspection: begins plain-text manifest, followed three concatenated H2 database segments. fully-formatted report Q-View renders user stored CLOB inside main H2 segment. parser scans binary CLOB, reassembles across H2 page boundaries (2048-byte pages), parses CSV. Parsing done pure R: Java runtime, H2 database driver, system dependencies beyond working R installation.

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   qv <- read_qview("plate.Q-View")   qv   qv$analytes   qv$pixel_intensities   plot(qv, type = "plate_map") } # }

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
code: read_qview_template(path, verbose = TRUE, call = rlang::caller_env())

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
text: path Path template file (csv). verbose Logical. Print short summary parsing. Default TRUE. call execution environment calling function. Used error reporting; experts .

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
text: tibble::tibble() one row per well columns well (character, e.g. "A1"), plate_row (character, ""..), plate_col (integer), sample_id (character), group_type (character), dilution (numeric).

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
text: Parses plate-template CSV Q-View imports sample assignment. file uses multi-section layout: NxM cell top-left declares plate dimensions, followed sections labelled Group Name, Group Type, Dilution Factor. section one row per plate row one column per plate column. template data also embedded inside .Q-View file (recovered read_qview()); function exists setting new plates cross-validating Q-View imports original template.

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   layout <- read_qview_template("plate-template.csv") } # }

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
code: strip_qview_prefix(x)

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
text: x Character vector Q-View internal names.

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
text: Character vector length x, prefixes stripped. NAs unrecognised values pass unchanged.

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
text: import, producing software prefixes well-assignment template names single-letter codes: "Cal 1" ... "Cal N" -> "ICal 1" ... "ICal N" (Internal calibrator) "Low" -> "GLow" "High" -> "HHigh" "FD..." -digit ID -> "N..." (sample prefix) strip_qview_prefix() reverses transformation downstream code sees identifiers exactly appear original template CSV. Strings match known prefix pattern returned unchanged.

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix

title: Summary statistics for a Q-View object — summary.qview
text: tibble::tibble() one row per well_type x analyte combination, columns: well_type, analyte, unit, n, mean, sd, cv, min, max.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
code: well_label(row, col, zero_based = FALSE)

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
text: row Integer character. Either 0/1-based row index already single letter (""...). col Integer column index (1-based 0-based; values >= 1 treated 1-based). zero_based Logical. TRUE, treat row col 0-based; FALSE (default), treat 1-based.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
text: Character vector well labels (e.g. "A1", "H12"), recycled longer row / col.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
text: Converts 0-based 1-based row column indices standard "A1" ... "H12" plate notation. Vectorised.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
code: well_label(0, 0, zero_based = TRUE)   # "A1" #> [1] "A1" well_label(7, 11, zero_based = TRUE)  # "H12" #> [1] "H12" well_label("C", 5)                    # "C5" #> [1] "C5"

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
code: write_qview_xlsx(   qv,   path,   template = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   call = rlang::caller_env() )  write_qview_csv(qv, path, template = NULL, call = rlang::caller_env())  write_qview_rds(qv, path, overwrite = FALSE, call = rlang::caller_env())  qview_to_xlsx(   qv,   path,   template = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   call = rlang::caller_env() )  qview_to_csv_dir(qv, dir, template = NULL, call = rlang::caller_env())

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
text: qv qview object read_qview(). path Output path. write_qview_xlsx() / write_qview_rds() single file path; write_qview_csv() output directory (created exist). template Optional plate-template tibble (read_qview_template()) include extra sheet / file. overwrite Logical. FALSE (default), existing destination triggers error; set TRUE replace . Ignored write_qview_csv() (adds files). call execution environment calling function. Used error reporting; experts . dir Deprecated alias path accepted qview_to_csv_dir(). Use path instead.

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
text: qv, invisibly, support pipelines.

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
text: Three writers, one common destination. return input qv invisibly compose pipe. write_qview_xlsx() – one sheet per parsed table. write_qview_csv()  – one CSV per parsed table inside directory. write_qview_rds()  – single .rds containing full qview object (lossless, round-trippable format).

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   qv <- read_qview("plate.Q-View")   qv |>     write_qview_xlsx("plate.xlsx") |>     write_qview_csv("plate_csv/")  |>     write_qview_rds("plate.rds") } # }

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: Major restructure: package now exclusively parser .Q-View binary project files (chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA plate imaging / quantification). previous panel-specific readers, normalisation, derivation, build helpers removed.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: read_qview(path, strip_prefix = FALSE): pure-R parser .Q-View container format. Returns list class qview containing project metadata, analyte panel units detection limits, well-group sample assignments, per-well replicate pixel intensities, summary statistics, optional back-calculated concentrations, curve fits, plate layout. strip_qview_prefix(): reverses Q-View’s internal naming convention (ICal N -> Cal N, GLow -> Low, HHigh -> High, NFD... / N1234... -> original ID). read_qview_template(): parser well-assignment template CSV (12x8 layout Group Name, Group Type, Dilution Factor sections). well_label(): vectorised plate-coordinate helper. print.qview() plot.qview(): compact summary quick-look plots (plate map, per-analyte intensity heatmap, replicate scatter). qview_to_xlsx() qview_to_csv_dir(): export parsed qview object multi-sheet workbook directory CSV files. qview_app(): Shiny front-end upload, parsing, preview, download.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: cp_read_complement(), cp_read_neuroaxonal(), cp_read_metadata(), cp_read_controls(), cp_read_auto() panel-specific fixtures tests. cp_build(), cp_normalize(), cp_derive(), cp_validate(), cp_summary(). cp_export_xlsx(), cp_export_csv(), cp_export_rds(). cp_example_data(), cp_example_files(), cp_timepoint_map(), cp_analyte_info().
